handler.description: Code reference (class name / image ref) the runtime instantiates.

protocols.description: Protocol bindings the agent is exposed on — HTTP, A2A, MCP, custom.

tools.description: Tools available to the agent. Each entry names a tool and optionally points at a source.

memory.description: Memory backing — pluggable store provider + connection ref. Null for ephemeral.

identity.description: Inbound/outbound auth configuration. Null for unauthenticated scenarios (dev, single-tenant).

autoscaling.description: Replica caps + target concurrency. Null for "whatever the runtime defaults to".

model.description: LLM model binding for declarative agents. Null when Handler carries custom behaviour. Set via object-initializer syntax: new AgentManifest(...) { Model = new ModelSpec(...) }.

systemPrompt.description: System prompt — inline text, template reference, or file reference. Exactly one shape set when non-null.

mcpServers.description: Model Context Protocol server bindings. Each server contributes tools to the agent at activation time.

a2aRemoteAgents.description: Agent2Agent (A2A) remote-agent bindings. Referenced by ToolRef.Source = "a2a:<name>". v0.17 Pillar B.

localAgents.description: Local (same-runtime) agent bindings. Referenced by ToolRef.Source = "agent:<name>". v0.18 — closes P7 agent-as-tool.

guardrails.description: Three-layer guardrail bindings (input / output / tool) — projected onto the equivalent StatefulAgentOptions fields at runtime.

handoffs.description: Declarative handoff targets — other agents this manifest can delegate to by id.

budget.description: Per-run budget caps — projected onto RunBudget at runtime.

contextProviders.description: Context providers bound to this agent — resolved against the host DI keyspace.

outputSchema.description: Structured-output shape for the final assistant turn. Inline JSON Schema.

agentMode.description: Execution-loop flavour — ToolCalling by default. Non-default values are contract-only in v0.6.

reasoning.description: Schema-Guided Reasoning configuration. Contract-only in v0.6 — engine treats as ToolCalling.

observability.description: Observability overlays — Langfuse project, sampling, custom tags.

llmGatewayRef.description: Optional reference to a deployed LlmGatewayConfigManifest by id. When set, the translator builds a per-agent LlmGatewayPipeline from the referenced config at grain activation, replacing (not appending to) the DI-global chain entirely. Null = DI-global chain applies unchanged (backwards compatible).

mcpGatewayRef.description: Optional reference to a deployed McpGatewayConfigManifest by id. When set, a per-agent ToolGatewayMiddleware chain is built from the referenced config at grain activation, replacing the DI-global chain entirely. Null = DI-global chain applies unchanged.

Agent.description: Declarative specification for an agent. The canonical shape for feeding an CancellationToken) call or for storing in an IAgentRegistry; deliberately minimal — the intersection of what every surveyed agent runtime requires (AWS Bedrock AgentCore, Dapr Agents, OpenAI Assistants, Temporal, Knative).

entry.description: Id of the entry node. Must exist in Nodes.

nodes.description: All nodes in the graph. Ids unique within the manifest.

edges.description: Edges between nodes, order-significant (first-match-wins when a node has multiple outgoing edges).

maxSteps.description: Hard ceiling on super-step count per invocation. Prevents runaway cycles. Defaults to 1000 (matching LangGraph's recursion_limit) when null. Exceeding this throws GraphRecursionException.

stateReducers.description: Per-key reducer overrides. Keys not listed here use the runtime defaults: messages appends, every other key last-write-wins. An explicit LastWriteWins entry overrides even the built-in messages append rule.

state.description: Optional JSON Schema for graph state (nested as spec.state.schema on the wire).

AgentGraph.description: Declarative specification for a graph of agents. Parallel to AgentManifest: one manifest = one addressable graph in the registry. Loaded from YAML/JSON under apiVersion: vais.agents/v1, kind: AgentGraph.

url.description: Server URL for streamableHttp / sse transports.

env.description: Environment variables passed to stdio child processes.

authRef.description: Optional secret:// URI for bearer / header auth on HTTP transports.

tools.description: Optional tool allowlist. Null = expose all tools the server lists.

container.description: Container spec for containerStdio transport. Required when Transport is containerStdio; must be null for other transports.

virtual.description: True = virtual aggregator. False (default) = physical server.

sources.description: Upstream server refs for virtual mode. Each Ref is an Id in IMcpServerRegistry.

toolProjection.description: Tool projection for virtual mode. Maps visible tool name → source server id. Null = expose all tools from all sources (de-duplicated by name, first source wins).

mcpGatewayRef.description: Optional Id applied per tool dispatch through this server. Lower precedence than an agent-level McpGatewayRef.

ontologyRef.description: Optional name of a deployment-supplied domain-ontology artifact to bind to this server. When set, the south cartridge resolves the artifact through IDomainOntologyArtifactRegistry and shapes the server's tools/list + tool-call dispatch with its tags, descriptions, and cross-refs. Missing or unknown refs degrade gracefully (no cartridge applied — passthrough). Plan C1-7.

McpServer.description: Registered MCP server — physical or virtual. Physical: wraps a single upstream server (same fields as McpServerRef plus registry metadata). Virtual: aggregates N upstream registered servers behind one logical name with optional tool projection (IBM Context Forge "virtual server" concept).

middleware.description: Ordered list of middleware layers. Execution order: index 0 = outermost interceptor. Each entry is resolved at activation time via ILlmGatewayMiddlewareFactory.

rateLimit.description: Optional rate-limit caps applied at the gateway level.

LlmGatewayConfig.description: Declarative specification for a named LLM gateway pipeline. Stored in ILlmGatewayConfigRegistry; referenced from LlmGatewayRef at agent activation time.

workspacePolicies.description: Per-workspace allow/deny tool policies. Keys are AgentContext.WorkspaceId values. Absent workspace → no policy constraint beyond the middleware chain.

McpGatewayConfig.description: Declarative specification for a named MCP/tool gateway pipeline. Stored in IMcpGatewayConfigRegistry; referenced from McpGatewayRef or McpGatewayRef.

build.description: Optional client-side build configuration for build-on-apply. Stored by the server but not acted upon at deploy time.

port.description: Port the container exposes for the IP-1 HTTP protocol. Default: 8080.

topology.description: Deployment topology: standalone | sidecar | kubernetes. Default: standalone.

startupTimeoutSeconds.description: Seconds to wait for the container health check on startup. Default: 30.

invokeTimeoutSeconds.description: Seconds allowed per invoke call before timing out. Default: 60.

sessionTtlSeconds.description: Full call-token lifetime in seconds for one invoke/session. When null, defaults to InvokeTimeoutSeconds + 30 (today's coupled behaviour). Set this to support a single long-lived invoke (e.g. a co-tenant coding session) without inflating the kill-timeout: in session mode the runtime issues short, renewable tokens whose validity is bound to a live invoke lease, up to this lifetime.

invokeIdleTimeoutSeconds.description: Idle/progress timeout in seconds for a streaming invoke: the runtime aborts the invoke if no streamed activity (a delta or an SSE heartbeat) arrives for this long, reclaiming a wedged container fast without capping a healthy long session. Applies to the /v1/stream path only (the non-streaming path has no liveness channel — long-lived plugins should stream). When null, no idle watchdog runs. Independent of the absolute cap, which is SessionTtlSeconds.

imagePullPolicy.description: Docker image pull policy: Always | IfNotPresent | Never. Default: IfNotPresent.

retryPolicy.description: Optional retry policy for failed invocations.

kubernetes.description: Kubernetes-specific configuration (required when Topology is kubernetes).

secrets.description: Secret references injected as environment variables. Key = env-var name; value = secret:// URI.

workspace.description: Opt-in writable workspace mount. Absent ⇒ today's behaviour (read-only rootfs plus the 64 MB ephemeral /tmp tmpfs only). When present, the runtime mounts one writable working tree at Path for the container's lifetime.

ContainerPlugin.description: Registered container plugin manifest. Describes a containerised agent handler that the runtime discovers via POST /v1/plugins (CLI apply) or filesystem scan at startup (legacy). Mirrors McpServerManifest in shape.

agentId.description: Optional id of the target agent to invoke for each case. Mutually exclusive with GraphId.

graphId.description: Optional id of the target graph to invoke for each case. Mutually exclusive with AgentId.

target.description: Structured target reference. When present, takes precedence over AgentId / GraphId.

defaults.description: Suite-level defaults applied to every case unless overridden.

baseline.description: Baseline run to diff against in vais eval diff.

cases.description: Test cases that make up this suite (batch mode). Exactly one of Cases or Sampling must be set. Null when Sampling is present.

replayMode.description: Suite-level replay mode default. Per-case Replay overrides this. Default: Live.

sampling.description: Continuous-mode sampling configuration. When set, exactly one of Cases or Sampling must be present. Mutually exclusive with Cases.

assertions.description: Suite-level assertion list used when Sampling is set (continuous mode). Each sampled production run is evaluated against all assertions here. Must be non-empty when Sampling is present.

EvalSuite.description: Declarative specification for an evaluation suite. Contains a named list of test cases that the eval runner executes against registered agents or graphs. Loaded via vais apply -f suite.yaml; stored in Orleans grain state (E1) or Postgres (E2+).
